tasks.subject: [error-categorization-retry] [P2] Replace raw error banner with structured TransportErrorBanner
tasks.description:
Replace the raw `
` error banner in ChatPanel.tsx (lines 353-357) with a structured banner that displays the `TransportErrorInfo` fields — heading, message, icon, and conditional retry button. ## 1. Update ChatPanel.tsx banner **File:** `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/ChatPanel.tsx` Add the `AlertTriangle` icon import (lucide-react is already used in this file for `ArrowDown`): ```typescript import { ArrowDown, AlertTriangle } from 'lucide-react'; ``` Import the `Button` component from shared UI: ```typescript import { Button } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; ``` Replace the existing error banner: ```tsx {/* Before: */} {error && (
{error}
)} {/* After: */} {error && (

{error.heading}

{error.message}

{error.retryable && ( )}
)} ``` Key design decisions: - Uses `border-destructive/30 bg-destructive/5` to match existing design system error styling (same as ErrorMessageBlock) - `AlertTriangle` icon consistent with ErrorMessageBlock - Heading in `font-medium text-sm text-destructive` - Message in `text-sm text-muted-foreground` - Retry button uses `outline` variant, `sm` size, only rendered when `error.retryable` is true - Button calls existing `handleRetry` callback (line 198-203 in ChatPanel) - Layout uses flexbox with `items-start` for multi-line alignment ## 2. Verify handleRetry works for transport errors The existing `handleRetry` (line 198-203) finds the last user message and calls `submitContent`. This works for transport errors because: - On transport error, the optimistic user message is removed from `messages` (catch block line 343-347 in use-chat-session.ts) - But the user's *previous* message (from a prior successful exchange) may exist - For a first message that fails, there's no prior user message, so `handleRetry` is a no-op This is acceptable behavior — the user can simply re-type and re-send. The retry button is most useful when an ongoing conversation hits a transient network/server error. ## 3. Verify SESSION_LOCKED auto-dismiss still works The `sessionBusy` state and its timer (`SESSION_BUSY_CLEAR_MS = 5000`) are separate from the `error` state. The `sessionBusy` auto-clear timer clears `sessionBusy` but does NOT clear `error`. We need to also clear `error` when sessionBusy clears. In `use-chat-session.ts`, update the sessionBusy timer callback: ```typescript sessionBusyTimerRef.current = setTimeout(() => { setSessionBusy(false); setError(null); // Clear the structured error along with busy state sessionBusyTimerRef.current = null; }, TIMING.SESSION_BUSY_CLEAR_MS); ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - Error banner shows AlertTriangle icon, heading, and message from TransportErrorInfo - Retry button appears only when `error.retryable` is true - Retry button calls `handleRetry` (re-sends last user message) - SESSION_LOCKED errors auto-dismiss when `sessionBusy` clears - Banner uses `border-destructive/30 bg-destructive/5` styling - No visual regression for existing error states - TypeScript compiles cleanly
tasks.activeForm: Replacing raw error banner with structured TransportErrorBanner
